comments.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->
<!-- CodeRabbit review command invocation: 2dfcaa2a-940b-4cc3-8862-519ae1f1ff3b -->
<details>
<summary>✅ Action performed</summary>

Review finished.

> Note: CodeRabbit is an incremental review system and does not re-review already reviewed commits. This command is applicable only when automatic reviews are paused.

</details>

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/Ahmad-mufied/monolith-vs-microservice-thesis/pull/68?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack) ## Walkthrough Introduces a unified `env/values.yaml.template` as the canonical configuration source for all deployment environments, replacing per-service `env/*.app.env` files. Adds YAML-reading helpers and Secret/ConfigMap split logic to `scripts/lib/shared-env.sh`, refactors all env-init and create-secrets scripts to read from `env/values.yaml`, adds `configMapRef` entries to all Kubernetes Deployment and Job manifests, and updates operator documentation throughout. ## Changes **Centralized values.yaml config system** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | |---|---| | **Unified config template and gitignore**
`.gitignore`, `env/values.yaml.template` | Adds `env/values.yaml.template` defining `local`, `compose`, `cluster`, and `shared` configuration sections for all services (ports, timeouts, DB URLs, JWT/bcrypt settings, feature flags). Ignores the generated `env/values.yaml` from version control. | | **shared-env.sh: YAML utilities and Secret/ConfigMap split**
`scripts/lib/shared-env.sh` | Adds `read_yaml_value` and `generate_env_from_yaml` YAML helpers; adds `is_sensitive_key` predicate and `deployment_runtime_configmap_name` mapping; replaces `secret_data_checksum` to include ConfigMap data in checksums; updates `apply_secret_from_pairs` to route sensitive vs. non-sensitive keys into Kubernetes Secrets vs. ConfigMaps; adds `apply_secret_and_config_from_env_file` entry point. | | **env-init scripts: YAML-driven initialization**
`scripts/env-init-app.sh`, `scripts/env-init-monolith.sh`, `scripts/env-init-microservices.sh` | `env-init-app.sh` replaces per-key env-file helpers with `migrate_value_to_yaml`, YAML-based JWT/password injection, and `generate_env_from_yaml` calls for all services. `env-init-monolith.sh` and `env-init-microservices.sh` become thin wrappers delegating to `shared-env.sh`. | | **create-\*-secrets scripts: consume env/values.yaml**
`scripts/create-local-secrets.sh`, `scripts/create-local-secrets-microservices.sh`, `scripts/create-eks-secrets-monolith.sh`, `scripts/create-eks-secrets-microservices.sh`, `scripts/create-eks-secrets-sequential.sh`, `scripts/create-vultr-secrets-monolith.sh`, `scripts/create-vultr-secrets-microservices.sh` | All create-secrets scripts replace per-service env file checks and `read_env_value` calls with `env/values.yaml` existence checks, `read_yaml_value` lookups, and delegate secret/ConfigMap application to `apply_secret_and_config_from_env_file` or `apply_secret_from_pairs`. | | **Kubernetes manifests: add configMapRef to all envFrom**
`deployments/k8s/cloud/...`, `deployments/k8s/local/...` | All Deployment and Job manifests for monolith and microservices (local and cloud) add a `configMapRef` entry alongside the existing `secretRef` under `envFrom`, wiring the new per-service ConfigMaps created by the updated secret scripts. | | **Documentation: values.yaml and ConfigMap partitioning**
`docs/infrastructure/secret-management.md`, `docs/infrastructure/vultr-operator-guide.md`, `docs/infrastructure/eks-sequential-runbook.md`, `docs/infrastructure/eks-debug-command-reference.md`, `docs/infrastructure/datadog.md` | Adds ConfigMap/Secret partitioning section and combined checksum annotation to `secret-management.md`; updates Vultr operator guide to reference `env/values.yaml` and ConfigMap queries; updates EKS runbook and debug reference for new file inputs and configmap commands; adds Datadog log-trace correlation documentation. | ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Operator participant env_init_app as env-init-app.sh participant shared_env as shared-env.sh participant values_yaml as env/values.yaml participant create_secrets as create-*-secrets.sh participant kubectl Operator->>env_init_app: make env-init env_init_app->>values_yaml: copy from template, inject JWT/passwords, replace DB URL placeholders env_init_app->>shared_env: generate_env_from_yaml (per service) shared_env->>values_yaml: read YAML section shared_env-->>env_init_app: write env/*.env (chmod 600) Operator->>create_secrets: make create-secrets create_secrets->>values_yaml: read_yaml_value (service config) create_secrets->>shared_env: apply_secret_and_config_from_env_file shared_env->>shared_env: is_sensitive_key → split into Secret / ConfigMap pairs shared_env->>kubectl: apply ConfigMap (non-sensitive keys) shared_env->>kubectl: apply Secret (sensitive keys) ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~60 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [Ahmad-mufied/monolith-vs-microservice-thesis#19](https://github.com/Ahmad-mufied/monolith-vs-microservice-thesis/pull/19): This PR's refactoring of `scripts/env-init-microservices.sh`, `scripts/env-init-monolith.sh`, and the addition of YAML-driven helpers in `scripts/lib/shared-env.sh` directly builds on the local env-init setup work introduced in PR `#19`. - [Ahmad-mufied/monolith-vs-microservice-thesis#33](https://github.com/Ahmad-mufied/monolith-vs-microservice-thesis/pull/33): Both PRs modify EKS secrets tooling around `create-eks-secrets-*` scripts and the Terraform/env-file workflow that this PR now redirects through `env/values.yaml`. - [Ahmad-mufied/monolith-vs-microservice-thesis#45](https://github.com/Ahmad-mufied/monolith-vs-microservice-thesis/pull/45): This PR updates `scripts/create-vultr-secrets-microservices.sh` and `scripts/create-vultr-secrets-monolith.sh` to source values from `env/values.yaml`, directly modifying the same Vultr VKE scripts introduced by PR `#45`. ## Poem > 🐇 Hop, hop, no more scattered `.env` files to chase, > One `values.yaml` now rules the whole place! > Secrets stay secret, ConfigMaps stay plain, > A `yq` path replaces the per-service pain. > The rabbit stamps checksums and rolls deployments neat — > With split keys and shared config, the cluster's complete! 🌿
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 4.55% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The PR title clearly summarizes the main change: consolidating configuration into values.yaml and partitioning Kubernetes ConfigMaps and Secrets by sensitivity. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
--- Thanks for using [CodeRabbit](https://coderabbit.ai?utm_source=oss&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=Ahmad-mufied/monolith-vs-microservice-thesis&utm_content=68)! It's free for OSS, and your support helps us grow. If you like it, consider giving us a shout-out.
❤️ Share - [X](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=I%20just%20used%20%40coderabbitai%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20the%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A&url=https%3A//coderabbit.ai) - [Mastodon](https://mastodon.social/share?text=I%20just%20used%20%40coderabbitai%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20the%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fcoderabbit.ai) - [Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/submit?title=Great%20tool%20for%20code%20review%20-%20CodeRabbit&text=I%20just%20used%20CodeRabbit%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A%20https%3A//coderabbit.ai) - [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcoderabbit.ai&mini=true&title=Great%20tool%20for%20code%20review%20-%20CodeRabbit&summary=I%20just%20used%20CodeRabbit%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20proprietary%20code)

latestReviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 8**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@docs/infrastructure/secret-management.md`:
- Around line 133-140: The Kubernetes Secret partitioning rule under
"Partitioning Rules" section 1 is missing the `*TOKEN*` pattern in its matcher
list. Add `*TOKEN*` to the documented matcher patterns (alongside `*SECRET*`,
`*PASSWORD*`, `*DATABASE_URL*`, and `*API_KEY*`) so that token-shaped keys like
`AUTH_TOKEN` are correctly classified as sensitive and stored in Kubernetes
Secrets rather than ConfigMaps. Ensure this pattern is also reflected in the
actual implementation of the `is_sensitive_key` matching logic used across all
the secret generation scripts (create-local-secrets.sh,
create-local-secrets-microservices.sh, create-eks-secrets-sequential.sh, etc.).

In `@scripts/create-eks-secrets-microservices.sh`:
- Around line 45-93: In all read_yaml_value function calls throughout the script
section, every hyphenated YAML path segment must be wrapped in double quotes to
ensure yq can correctly resolve them. Specifically, in the paths passed to
read_yaml_value, change unquoted segments like k6-runner, api-gateway,
auth-service, item-service, and transaction-service to their quoted equivalents:
"k6-runner", "api-gateway", "auth-service", "item-service", and
"transaction-service" respectively. Apply this quoting pattern consistently
across all read_yaml_value invocations in the configuration variable assignments
to prevent JWT secrets and admin credentials from remaining unset.

In `@scripts/create-eks-secrets-monolith.sh`:
- Around line 33-37: The hyphenated YAML key k6-runner is not quoted in the yq
path expressions passed to read_yaml_value, causing yq to misinterpret it as a
subtraction operation rather than a literal key name. For both ADMIN_USER_EMAIL
and ADMIN_USER_PASSWORD variable assignments where read_yaml_value is called
with the path arguments, wrap the k6-runner portion of the path in double quotes
so it is treated as a literal key name by yq. Apply this same fix to the
corresponding variable assignments in the other affected files:
scripts/create-eks-secrets-microservices.sh,
scripts/create-eks-secrets-sequential.sh,
scripts/create-vultr-secrets-monolith.sh, and
scripts/create-vultr-secrets-microservices.sh.

In `@scripts/create-eks-secrets-sequential.sh`:
- Around line 33-106: Throughout the variable assignment section, all calls to
the read_yaml_value function need to have their hyphenated YAML key segments
properly quoted. Locate each read_yaml_value call and ensure that any key
segment containing hyphens (such as k6-runner, api-gateway, auth-service, and
transaction-service) is wrapped in double quotes within the YAML path argument.
This applies to all instances across the admin user section, monolith section,
API Gateway section, Auth Service section, Item Service section, and Transaction
Service section to ensure yq can parse the paths correctly.

In `@scripts/create-local-secrets-microservices.sh`:
- Around line 60-61: The variable auth_login_max_concurrency_hpa on this line is
missing a default fallback value, unlike other similarly defined variables in
this section. Add a bash parameter expansion fallback to this variable
assignment using the same pattern as auth_login_max_concurrency (the variable
should have a format like "${auth_login_max_concurrency_hpa:-DEFAULT_VALUE}") to
ensure it has a sensible default when both the environment variable and YAML
value are missing, preventing empty values from being passed to the secret.

In `@scripts/create-vultr-secrets-microservices.sh`:
- Around line 63-111: In all read_yaml_value() function calls throughout the
configuration variable assignments, hyphenated YAML keys must be quoted to
prevent yq from interpreting hyphens as operators. Locate all instances where
read_yaml_value() is called with paths containing hyphenated keys (api-gateway,
auth-service, item-service, transaction-service, and k6-runner) and wrap each
hyphenated key segment in double quotes within the path string. For example,
change paths like ".cluster.microservices.api-gateway.JWT_SECRET" to use
".cluster.microservices."api-gateway".JWT_SECRET" format, applying this same
pattern consistently across all service configuration paths.

In `@scripts/create-vultr-secrets-monolith.sh`:
- Around line 60-64: The YAML paths in the read_yaml_value function calls
contain unquoted hyphenated keys (k6-runner) which yq interprets as subtraction
operators, causing parsing failures. In both the admin_user_email and
admin_user_password variable assignments where read_yaml_value is called with
paths ".shared.k6-runner.ADMIN_USER_EMAIL" and
".shared.k6-runner.ADMIN_USER_PASSWORD", quote the hyphenated key portion by
changing ".shared.k6-runner" to ".shared."k6-runner"" in both YAML path
arguments to ensure yq parses the keys correctly.

In `@scripts/lib/shared-env.sh`:
- Around line 59-61: The yq expression in the command that generates the
environment file concatenates .key with "=" and .value directly, but if .value
is a non-string type (number or boolean), the concatenation will fail or produce
malformed output. Modify the yq expression to coerce .value to a string using
the tostring function before concatenating with the key and equals sign, so that
values like 25 or false are properly converted to their string representations
"25" and "false" in the environment file.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🪄 Autofix (Beta)</summary>

Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR:

- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "4b0d0e0a-96d7-4f10-b296-3a18ea78f0b9"} --> Push a commit to this branch (recommended)
- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "ff5b1114-7d8c-49e6-8ac1-43f82af23a33"} --> Create a new PR with the fixes

</details>

---

<summary>ℹ️ Review info</summary>

<details>
<summary>⚙️ Run configuration</summary>

**Configuration used**: Repository UI

**Review profile**: CHILL

**Plan**: Pro Plus

**Run ID**: `d08a9996-9a25-40d8-86b5-07b598fbee47`

</details>

<details>
<summary>📥 Commits</summary>

Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between 5afc03a689f0361c962c229a6d2c90e6aecf0b7b and b8d0c9dc698bd9c069ef65d37d79440a1f7f258c.

</details>

<details>
<summary>📒 Files selected for processing (36)</summary>

* `.gitignore`
* `deployments/k8s/cloud/microservices/auth-migration-job.yaml`

* `deployments/k8s/local/microservices/item-migration-job.yaml`
* `deployments/k8s/local/microservices/item-service.yaml`
* `deployments/k8s/local/microservices/transaction-migration-job.yaml`
* `deployments/k8s/local/microservices/transaction-service.yaml`

* `env/values.yaml.template`
* `scripts/create-eks-secrets-microservices.sh`
* `scripts/create-eks-secrets-monolith.sh`
* `scripts/create-eks-secrets-sequential.sh`
* `scripts/create-local-secrets-microservices.sh`
* `scripts/create-local-secrets.sh`
* `scripts/create-vultr-secrets-microservices.sh`
* `scripts/create-vultr-secrets-monolith.sh`
* `scripts/env-init-app.sh`
* `scripts/env-init-microservices.sh`
* `scripts/env-init-monolith.sh`
* `scripts/lib/shared-env.sh`

</details>

</details>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit for review status -->
